project: Control and identification of dissipative dynamics in open quantum systems
notes: This file is a local citation seed, not a complete bibliography.
Do not cite a reference in a manuscript until metadata has been checked against DOI, publisher, arXiv, or official documentation.
The deep-research PDF was used as a planning input, not as a primary source.

references.citation: G. Lindblad, On the generators of quantum dynamical semigroups, Communications in Mathematical Physics 48, 119-130 (1976).
references.why_it_matters: Foundational GKSL generator result for Markovian quantum dynamical semigroups.

references.citation: V. Gorini, A. Kossakowski, and E. C. G. Sudarshan, Completely positive dynamical semigroups of N-level systems, Journal of Mathematical Physics 17, 821-825 (1976).
references.why_it_matters: Companion mathematical structure for completely positive semigroups.

references.citation: H.-P. Breuer and F. Petruccione, The Theory of Open Quantum Systems, Oxford University Press (2002).
references.why_it_matters: Standard reference for derivations, approximations, and validity limits of master equations.

references.citation: H. Spohn, Entropy production for quantum dynamical semigroups, Journal of Mathematical Physics 19, 1227-1230 (1978).
references.why_it_matters: Foundational entropy-production result for quantum dynamical semigroups with stationary states.

references.citation: J. Goold, M. Huber, A. Riera, L. del Rio, and P. Skrzypczyk, The role of quantum information in thermodynamics - a topical review, Journal of Physics A 49, 143001 (2016).
references.why_it_matters: Connects quantum information concepts such as entropy and relative entropy with thermodynamics.

references.citation: F. A. Bonk et al., Quantum-state tomography for quadrupole nuclei and its applications on a two-qubit system, Physical Review A 69, 042322 (2004).
references.why_it_matters: Platform-specific tomography reference for quadrupolar spin systems.

references.citation: F. A. Bonk et al., Quantum logical operations for spin 3/2 quadrupolar nuclei monitored by quantum state tomography, Journal of Magnetic Resonance 175, 226-234 (2005).
references.why_it_matters: Directly relevant spin-3/2 quadrupolar NMR reference for Hamiltonian, tomography, and logical operations.

references.citation: J. Teles et al., Quantum state tomography for quadrupolar nuclei using global rotations of the spin system, Journal of Chemical Physics 126, 154506 (2007).
references.why_it_matters: Alternative tomography protocol for quadrupolar nuclei.

references.citation: Z. Hradil, Quantum-state estimation, Physical Review A 55, R1561 (1997).
references.why_it_matters: Foundational reference for maximum-likelihood quantum-state estimation.

references.citation: D. F. V. James, P. G. Kwiat, W. J. Munro, and A. G. White, Measurement of qubits, Physical Review A 64, 052312 (2001).
J. Zhang and M. Sarovar, Identification of open quantum systems from observable time traces, Physical Review A 91, 052121 (2015).
references.why_it_matters: Directly relevant to parameter identification from observable time traces.

references.citation: N. Khaneja et al., Design of NMR pulse sequences by gradient ascent algorithms, Journal of Magnetic Resonance 172, 296-305 (2005).
references.why_it_matters: Standard GRAPE reference for NMR pulse-sequence optimization.

references.citation: C. Brif, R. Chakrabarti, and H. Rabitz, Control of quantum phenomena: past, present and future, New Journal of Physics 12, 075008 (2010).
references.why_it_matters: Broad review of quantum control concepts and applications.

references.citation: E. Magesan, J. M. Gambetta, and J. Emerson, Scalable and robust randomized benchmarking of quantum processes, Physical Review Letters 106, 180504 (2011).
references.why_it_matters: Standard randomized benchmarking reference for hardware-facing validation.

references.citation: E. Nielsen et al., Gate Set Tomography, Quantum 5, 557 (2021).
J. R. Johansson, P. D. Nation, and F. Nori, QuTiP 2: A Python framework for the dynamics of open quantum systems, Computer Physics Communications 184, 1234-1240 (2013).
references.why_it_matters: External reference implementation for open-system dynamics.

references.citation: C. R. Harris et al., Array programming with NumPy, Nature 585, 357-362 (2020).
references.why_it_matters: Standard citation for NumPy-based scientific computing.
